title: Test script depends on an undeclared Bun runtime
reasoning: The declared project command is `npm run test`, but that script shells out to `bun test`. `package.json` does not declare a Bun package manager version or runtime requirement, so a normal Node/npm checkout can install dependencies successfully and still fail to run tests with `bun: command not found`. This makes test execution environment-dependent and can break CI or release validation.
reproduction: On a machine with Node/npm but without Bun installed, run `npm install` and then `npm run test`; the script fails before running tests because `bun` is unavailable.
recommendation: Declare the Bun requirement explicitly, for example with a `packageManager` field such as `bun@<version>` and CI/setup documentation, or change the test script to a Node/npm-compatible runner if Bun is not required for the project.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: This is a package/runtime contract issue; project tests only run after the package script successfully resolves the `bun` executable.
minimumFixScope: Update `package.json` to declare the required package/runtime contract or replace the test script with a runner available from declared npm dependencies.

history.reasoning: The original compact package.json evidence has moved but still exists: package.json lines 9-14 define scripts.test as "bun test" and other scripts also invoke bun. Current package.json has no packageManager, engines, or devEngines field; `npm pkg get scripts.test packageManager engines devEngines` returns only {"scripts.test":"bun test"}. There is a bun.lock and docs/PRD.md says testing uses Bun, but that is not an npm-install/runtime contract for a normal Node/npm checkout. Running `npm run test` in this environment found local Bun at /Users/bjesuiter/.bun/bin/bun and started `bun test`; it failed later because the read-only sandbox blocks temp-directory creation, not because the runtime contract is fixed. The undeclared Bun runtime dependency is therefore still open.
commands: command -v bun; bun --version; npm run test
npm pkg get scripts.test packageManager engines devEngines

history.reasoning: The original evidence at package.json line 1 is stale because package.json is no longer compact and now explicitly declares the Bun runtime contract. Current package.json lines 5-8 contain "packageManager": "bun@1.3.14" and engines.bun ">=1.3.14", while lines 14-19 still define scripts.test as "bun test". `npm pkg get scripts.test packageManager engines devEngines` confirms scripts.test, packageManager, and engines are present. `npm run test` resolves local Bun 1.3.14 and starts `bun test`; failures are EPERM temp-directory creation errors from the read-only sandbox, not `bun: command not found`. The undeclared Bun runtime issue is fixed.
commands: npm pkg get scripts.test packageManager engines devEngines
command -v bun; bun --version; npm run test
